candidate_grammar.boundary: All finite representations generated from complete canonical source alphabets, generated Fold codewords, total maps, inverse relations, retained source classes and exact interface composition.
candidate_grammar.generator: Generate the complete product of source coverage, code support, encoding totality, single-valuedness, provenance, decoder law, exact/lossy status, composition, finite generality and extra-code status.

excluded_inputs: no infinite codebook as a completed object
intended_certificate: Independent regeneration of the complete 1024-candidate product, its sole all-preserving survivor, closure properties and controls.
statement: An admitted encoding is a total single-valued source-bound map from a complete canonical source alphabet into generated codeword support, retaining every source-to-code relation. Exact decoding exists exactly when every used codeword class contains one source and the decoder is the resulting left inverse. A many-to-one encoding is lawful only as declared lossy representation whose complete closed-source classes remain recorded.
title: Exact encoding, decoding and declared representational loss
